rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SAN LEE AND MUI CHOW LEE REVOCABLE TRUST U/D/T DATED MARCH 24, 1985 AS AMENDED 12/22/2004
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/2/23. (No earlier available date.) Examiner needs more time to complete review. No proposed order appears to have been submitted yet. No financial statements appear to have been filed yet. The amended fourth accounting ended on 10/7/20. Why does this one begin on 10/9/20? There was $766.17 in cash on hand as of 10/7/20. This one starts with $757.05. It appears that all of the funds exceeding the FDIC limit are being held in one checking account. The calculation shown in paragraph 9 of the petition does not appear to be correct, and the $2,500 "income" shown in the accounting was reportedly cash advanced by the trustee to cover expenses, not truly income. There should now be interest, however, from the cash in the estate... The terms of the trust for Brian Leland Chow do not appear to have been set forth in the petition. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Why was notice given to the buyer of the real property and the real estate agents? The property was already sold.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAUREEN E. CROWE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/2/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. [Probate Code Section 12201.] (2) The petition prayer still has not been supplemented to set forth the amount of additional time for estate administration being sought. Further "status reports" by the attorney are NOT adequate. A petition must be either supplemented or amended by the fiduciary. [CRC 7.3 and 7.103(c).] (3) Nothing appears to have been filed yet to address the apparent conflict of the attorney, who previously represented the petitioning beneficiaries, now representing the executor. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/20/23. NB: If the estate is not ready to be closed, an accounting will need to be filed soon. [LRSF 14.34 A.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSANNE CAPALBO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/2/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The Final I&A filed on 1/13/23 showed $95,292.83 in cash on page 1, but the cash items on Attachment 1 totaled $95,928.83. The administrator states in paragraph 5 of her amended petition that there was just $11,834.80 in cash on hand on the date of death. (2) What does it mean that the residence had "by then been sold", as reported in paragraph 5? By when? It was reported on the I&A. (3) The amended petition still does not fully disclose actions taken by the administrator. The assets on hand differ from the assets on the date of death and not just because of a variation in values. [CRC 7.550(b)(2).] (4) The proposed distribution must state the complete DESCRIPTION OF EACH INDIVIDUAL SECURITY and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) DISTRIBUTABLE TO EACH BENEFICIARY. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] In other words, if there are securities still on hand, the proposed distribution must show the NUMBER OF SHARES of each holding distributable to each heir, not the total current value of all assets on hand, as securities values change constantly. NOT "in-kind" but SPECIFIC assets. (5) The administrator still asserts, simply, that she retained cash in an estate bank account, not fully addressing Probate Code Section 1064(a)(5). Subparagraph c of Part 1 of the Duties and Liabilities specifically states that, except for checking accounts used for ordinary estate administration expenses, estate accounts must earn interest and funds must be in insured accounts. (6) Paragraph 20 of the amended is still untrue. There WAS a creditor's claim filed on 4/13/21. What action was taken on the claim? (7) How was the "net estate value" determined, as shown in paragraph 25 of the amended petition? Please review CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6). (The administrator and her attorney might want to double-check the math.) (8) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. It appears that an amendment to the amended petition must be filed. Notice must be given of the amendment after it is e-filed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/21/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL DORIA PALACIOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although some of the dollar amounts in the second amendment appear to be incorrect (e.g., the $617.87 in costs advanced by Administrator subtracted from the $1,000 shown in paragraph vi of section I and two different figures for the cash on hand in section II). Notice of the second amendment was mailed to a different address for each heir without any explanation. This Court does not ordinarily order a time by when, or after which, distribution must be made, unless such time frame was part of a settlement agreement; distribution from a decedent's estate must always be made promptly when ordered. There was nothing in the petition or amendments that mentioned a reserve for taxes or closing expenses, and no reserve was requested. (Pleading forms of proof of service should not be filed in Probate proceedings unless a copy of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing is attached.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH ALLEN SIEVERTSEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/2/23. (No earlier date available.) (1) No proof of service of the Notice of Hearing has been filed. (2) Two "corrections" were filed on 1/31/23. The one changing the proposed distribution was an amendment to the petition. (The legislature has recognized only supplements or amendments to petitions. [CRC 7.3.]) The amendment to the proposed distribution also requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] (3) No courtesy copies of the petition and the subsequently filed papers were received by the assigned examiner, which delayed review. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] The hearing was originally going to be continued because of the erroneous allegation regarding creditor claims; the "declaration" filed on 1/31/23 (which was also an amendment), however, amended that allegation. (4) No Allowance of the claim was filed, however. [Probate Code Section 9250(b).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (There is no longer a state estate tax.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MATTHEW AND MELISSA WHITE 2007 LIFE INSURANCE TRUST AGREEMENT DATED NOVEMBER 7, 2007
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Modifying Trust To Change Beneficiaries Based On Consent Of Trustors And Beneficiaries
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Counsel may address to the court (1) if the trustee's power authorized pursuant to Art. IX.(q). "Division of Trust" is exercisable or not (2) if the two new trusts are granted, whether the existing 2007 ILT will remain operative. If not, the petition appears to seek termination of the existing 2007 ILT. Otherwise, the existing 2007 ILT may need to be modified to include (not replaced with) the entire two new trusts. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/11/2023. A photocopy of documents attached to Declaration filed 2/3/2023 insufficient pursuant to PC 12521. The following documents must have the original seals (embossed if applicable) thereon. Therefore, the documents will need to be submitted by mail or in person: (1) Without any coversheet, an authenticated (certified) copy of the decedent's will that was admitted in the sister state. (2) With a coversheet (or a declaration), an authenticated copy of the order admitting the will to probate in the sister state.
